prompt: Publish this HTML file at /tmp/report.html and give me the URL
expected_output: Agent runs dropthis publish with --url flag and returns the URL from stdout
expectations: Uses `dropthis publish /tmp/report.html --url`
Uses --url flag to get just the URL (not --json)
Returns the URL from stdout to the user
Does NOT parse JSON output -- --url gives a plain URL string

prompt: Publish my dist folder with a custom title 'Preview Deploy'
expected_output: Agent runs dropthis publish with --title and --url flags
expectations: Uses `dropthis publish ./dist --title "Preview Deploy" --url`
Includes --title flag with the specified title
Uses --url to get the published URL back
Passes the directory path directly (not individual files)

prompt: Update drop_abc123 with the new content in ./dist-v2
expected_output: Agent uses drops update command (not publish) with the drop id
expectations: Uses `dropthis drops update drop_abc123 ./dist-v2 --url`
Uses `drops update`, NOT standalone `update` or `publish`
Passes the drop id as the first argument
Passes the new content path as the second argument

expected_output: Agent uses drops delete with --yes flag since it's running non-interactively
expectations: Uses `dropthis drops delete drop_abc123 --yes`
Includes --yes flag because agents run in non-TTY (non-interactive) mode
Does NOT omit --yes and expect a confirmation prompt

prompt: I'm not logged in, help me authenticate with dropthis
expected_output: Agent checks auth status with whoami, then guides through the two-step non-interactive login flow
expectations: Runs `dropthis whoami --json` first to check current auth status
Checks the `authenticated` field in the response
If not authenticated, asks the user for their email address
Runs `dropthis login request --email <email>` to send OTP
Tells user to check their inbox for the code
After user provides code, runs `dropthis login verify --email <email> --otp <code>`
Does NOT attempt interactive `dropthis login` since agents are non-TTY

prompt: Publish this report privately with a password 'quarterly2025'
expectations: Uses `dropthis publish <file> --password quarterly2025 --url`
Includes --password flag with the specified password
May also include --visibility unlisted for additional privacy
Uses --url to return the URL

prompt: Set up dropthis in my CI/CD pipeline so it publishes the dist folder on every deploy
expected_output: Agent instructs to use DROPTHIS_API_KEY env var, not interactive login
expectations: Recommends setting DROPTHIS_API_KEY as a CI/CD secret
Shows `export DROPTHIS_API_KEY=sk_live_...` or equivalent CI config
Uses `dropthis publish ./dist --url` as the publish command
Does NOT recommend `dropthis login` for CI -- uses env var auth
Mentions that non-TTY environments auto-output JSON

prompt: Generate a landing page for my project 'Acme Widgets' and publish it live
expected_output: Agent writes HTML to a temp file, then publishes with dropthis
expectations: Generates HTML content for the landing page
Writes HTML to a file (e.g., /tmp/landing.html)
Runs `dropthis publish /tmp/landing.html --url --title "Acme Widgets"`
Returns the live URL to the user
Does NOT try to pass HTML inline as a CLI argument
Uses `dropthis drops list`
Uses the `drops list` subcommand, not a different command
Output will be JSON with an array of drops

prompt: Publish from stdin: I want to pipe this HTML string into dropthis
expected_output: Agent pipes content to stdin; --content-type/--path are optional (auto-detected), recommended for determinism
expectations:
Pipes content to dropthis, e.g., `echo '

Hello

' | dropthis publish - --url` (bare stdin is accepted)
Uses `-` as the input argument, or omits the input entirely so stdin is read automatically
MAY add `--content-type text/html` and `--path index.html` for deterministic output (the SDK auto-detects them if omitted)
Does NOT claim --content-type or --path are required for stdin
Does NOT save the piped content to a temp file first just to publish it

prompt: Publish these three files together: index.html, styles.css, and app.js
expected_output: Agent passes all three files as arguments to publish
expectations: Uses `dropthis publish index.html styles.css app.js --url`
Passes all three files as SEPARATE arguments to one publish command
All files are bundled into one drop automatically (multi-arg bundle)
Does NOT inline styles.css or app.js into index.html
Does NOT create a directory, zip, or base64 the files first
Does NOT run publish three separate times

prompt: The publish command failed with exit code 3, what should I do?
expected_output: Agent explains that exit 3 means auth required and guides the user to authenticate
expectations: Identifies exit code 3 as auth_error
Suggests checking auth with `dropthis whoami --json`
Recommends authenticating via `dropthis login` or setting DROPTHIS_API_KEY
Reads the `next_action` field from the error JSON for guidance
Does NOT suggest random fixes unrelated to authentication

prompt: Publish a copy of the page at https://example.com/report.html and give me the URL
expected_output: Agent passes the URL directly to publish (source_url flow), does not fetch it first
expectations: Runs `dropthis https://example.com/report.html --url` (or `dropthis publish https://example.com/report.html --url`)
Passes the URL string directly as the publish input — the server fetches it (source_url)
Does NOT curl/wget/fetch the URL and save it to a file first
Does NOT treat the URL as inline HTML content
